hypothesis: Incorporating a persistent, queryable knowledge-graph memory that encodes force-field entity–relation schemas, RASPA keyword semantics, and episodic setup outcomes into the multi-agent framework will reduce wrong-parameter assignments in literature extraction by at least 50% (increasing mean IoU from ~0.90 to ≥0.95) and halve simulation-setup misconfigurations (e.g., mixture vs single-adsorbate, incorrect Widom settings) on previously unseen multi-adsorbate and multi-structure tasks relative to the current prompt-only system.
